headline: V Stock: Where Compounding Could Take The Price
summary: Visa (V) trades at $360.57 per share on a $651.9B market cap and 29.3x trailing earnings. Under a conservative 3-year scenario, the math points to roughly 42% of upside. Revenue compounding does most of the work in our scenario. Here is the picture the math sits on top of.

headline: 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy Before July Ends and Hold Forever
summary: Three dividend growers with very different setups are quietly positioning long-term investors for decades of rising income, but each one carries a specific risk in the second half of 2026 that could shake out impatient holders before the real compounding begins.

headline: Visa (V) Launches A Stablecoin Platform For Banks And Fintechs
summary: Visa launches the Visa Stablecoin Platform, giving financial institutions and fintechs a dedicated enterprise platform for stablecoin access and management. The new platform supports onchain wallet infrastructure, fiat on and off ramping, and integration into existing Visa payment flows. This move extends Visa’s crypto and digital asset efforts, aiming to make stablecoins easier to use for treasury, settlement, and new product offerings. Visa, traded on the NYSE under the ticker NYSE:V, is...
